expectations.text: 在 docs/deeper_userNote/test-ecs/ 下创建了多个 .md 文件 (adapted: check outputs_dir for multiple .md files)
README.md 包含章节导航表格
expectations.evidence: README.md lines 18-25 contain a Markdown table with columns (#, 章节, 文件, 内容) listing all 6 chapters with descriptions and file links

expectations.text: 覆盖了笔记中提出的 3 个问题（System 依赖、EnTT vs Flecs、Unity 区别）
expectations.evidence: README.md lines 28-31 explicitly maps all three questions to chapters: question 1 (System依赖) → chapter 4, question 2 (EnTT vs Flecs) → chapter 5, question 3 (Unity区别) → chapter 6. All three chapters contain substantive content addressing each question.

All 6 content chapters contain code blocks: 01 (C++ entity/component/system defs, map approach, entity-as-index), 02 (C++ diamond problem, composition, OOP vs ECS comparison), 03 (ASCII diagrams of memory layouts), 04 (C++ EnTT flow, Flecs pipeline), 05 (C++ EnTT API, Flecs C++/C API), 06 (C# MonoBehaviour, C# DOTS ISystem, C++ component assembly)

expectations.text: 内容包含联网搜索来源引用
expectations.evidence: README.md has a '来源' section with 8 URLs (Wikipedia, skypjack blog, EnTT/Flecs GitHub, Sander Mertens medium, Game Programming Patterns). Chapters 01, 02, 03, 05, 06 each have their own '来源' section with specific URLs. Chapter 04 (System依赖) is the only chapter without an explicit sources section, but the overall document collection contains extensive web citations.
6 content chapters (01 through 06), which is within the 3-6 range

claims.claim: 基于笔记 drafts/test-ecs.md 深化而成
claims.evidence: Source note file (drafts/test-ecs.md) was not found in the workspace; cannot verify the content was derived from it
Stated in chapter 5 table; cannot independently verify from available outputs
Stated in chapter 6; plausible but cannot verify from available outputs alone

claims.claim: skypjack says both libraries are 'in the same league'
claims.evidence: Source URL cited (ECS Back and Forth Part 2) supports this high-level characterization; sentiment consistent with the cited blog series

suggestions.reason: This assertion only checks chapter count, not content depth or correctness. A set of 6 empty or nonsense .md files would also pass. Consider adding assertions about minimum content length, presence of specific technical depth markers (e.g., storage model comparison, performance characteristics), or correctness of key claims.

suggestions.assertion: 内容包含联网搜索来源引用
suggestions.reason: Chapter 04 (System依赖) lacks its own source citations while every other chapter has them. The assertion passes because other chapters + README have sources, but a stronger assertion would require each chapter to cite its own sources.

No assertion checks whether the outputs actually deepen the analysis beyond surface-level summaries. The content is substantive (code examples, comparison tables, implementation evolution), but the evals don't measure depth — they only check structural presence.

eval_feedback.overall: All 6 expectations pass with clear evidence. The assertions cover structure (file count, table, chapter count) and content presence (topic coverage, code, citations) but do not verify depth, correctness, or that the material advances beyond what a quick web search would return. Consider adding assertions for specific technical claims (e.g., 'correctly identifies Sparse Set as EnTT's storage model') and minimum per-chapter depth indicators.
